arts.ttl: Leicester v Brentford: Premier League – live
arts.sum: Updates from King Power Stadium, 8pm GMT kick-off Get in touch with Dominic here | Read Football Daily Am I naive in predicting that this game will perhaps not the foregone conclusion the form-book suggests? Leicester really need this; like really need this. Brentford aren’t quite on the proverbial beach as yet, but they seem safely ensconced in mid-table and probably won’t trouble the European places. Van Nistelrooy has looked a little lost in the King Power dugout since replacing Steve Cooper, but if he can’t fire up his troops for a game like tonight’s, he’s in the wrong job. arts.ttl: Leicester have made player ‘one of their priorities’ for summer – Already planning for transfer window
arts.sum: Leicester City are targeting a move for Borussia Dortmund’s Soumaïla Coulibaly as a potential replacement for Caleb Okoli. That’s according to Africa Foot, who report that Leicester are already planning for the summer transfer window and have the 21-year-old in their sights. arts.ttl: Leicester City join ‘top clubs’ in race for signing – Player wants summer move
arts.sum: Last month, we covered claims from Germany which stated Leicester City were eyeing a move for VfL Wolfsburg’s Patrick Wimmer in the winter market. The Premier League club weren’t able to secure his services and are still in the picture to sign him. arts.ttl: Brentford XI vs Leicester – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Brentford head to struggling Leicester tonight looking to continue their impressive away form. Thomas Frank’s team have won each of their last three Premier League games on the road. arts.ttl: Leicester vs Brentford – Predicted lineup and team news
arts.sum: Leicester can climb out of the Premier League’s bottom three with a win against Brentford on Friday night. The Foxes kick off the top-flight weekend when hosting the Bees at the King Power Stadium this evening. arts.ttl: West Ham and Leicester City want to sign same player – Have made ‘unofficial offers’
arts.sum: West Ham United and Leicester City are in the picture to sign Club Brugge’s Chemsdine Talbi in the summer window. That’s at least what Africa Foot are reporting, and they cover the latest on West Ham and Leicester’s efforts to try and sign the 19-year-old. arts.ttl: Leicester City must address record-breaking problem for Brentford with Nigel Pearson approach - Josh Holland - Leicestershire Live
arts.sum: Should van Nistelrooy's men fail to score and lose again on Friday, they will become the first team in top-flight history to go six in a row in the same season. Leicester City kick off a crucial period in their fight to stay in the Premier League this evening when they have the advantage of playing before their rivals kick a ball.
